planner.description: breaks any goal into tiny next steps

memory-coach.description: gives one ADHD-friendly reframe or trick

demo-echo.description: test agent that echoes what you send it

ops.description: ops agent (example remote agent - move to VPS)

bookkeeper.description: the user's MEMORY: stores and recalls the user's tasks, schedule, appointments, notes and preferences. Route here anything about what the user has to do, has planned, or has told us.

browser.description: operates the LIVE WEB through a real stealth browser: looks up current facts/news/prices/weather, reads any website, AND logs into and acts on the user's web apps — WhatsApp Web, Gmail and other email, Slack, Notion, Google, calendars, etc. (open, read, click, type, fill forms). Route here anything needing the live web or any online app/service.

orchestrator.description: MANAGER: handles multi-step 'build/make/do X' requests, coordinates several agents end to end, and is the escalation point when no single agent fits.

builder.description: BUILDS new agents on demand (a new Hermes agent with its own brain, instantly callable).

repair.description: diagnoses and FIXES the network: revives dead agents and brains from afar (wake_agent/wake_all_down, clears stale locks), recreates the stealth browser, restarts the terra proxy. Route here anything like 'X is down / not answering / broken'.

toolsmith.description: FORGES deterministic API tools and attaches each to the right specialist agent.

router.description: ask me when you're unsure which agent should handle something or you can't do it yourself; I route your request to the right agent and return its answer.

audit.description: reviews how the network performs (each action vs its ask, latency, failures), REVIVES dead or unresponsive agents itself (wake/restart services and brains, even remotely), and requests surgical improvements — tools from the toolsmith, agents from the builder
